event_headline: Philippine Solicitor General urges Supreme Court to allow ICC arrest of Sen. Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa

chronology.action: submitted its comment on two petitions filed by the camp of Sen. Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa before the Supreme Court
urged the Supreme Court to deny petitions of Senator Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa

chronology.actor: new lawyer of former President Rodrigo Duterte
chronology.action: requested a re-examination of his health and capacity to face trial before setting a date for the start of the ICC trial
eyes 20-30 witnesses to testify orally in Duterte trial and has over 13,000 evidence still pending review
rejected former President Rodrigo Duterte’s request to appeal the confirmation of charges against him
tracker teams stated readiness to pursue former agency chief Senator Ronald dela Rosa
stated that several more individuals may soon face arrest warrants from the ICC
insisted that the warrant of arrest against Sen. Bato dela Rosa cannot be served by a lawyer
stated that it is wrong to call his client, Sen. Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa, a 'fugitive from justice'
called the war on drugs of former President Rodrigo Duterte's administration a 'pro-life campaign'
made a 'bad joke' about Sen. Bato dela Rosa getting arrested by the ICC

chronology.actor: National Union of Journalists of the Philippines (NUJP)
chronology.action: called for justice for the slain radio broadcaster Nestor Micator
called for justice for the victims of the war on drugs and the arrest of Sen. Bato dela Rosa

endorsed to the Department of Justice (DOJ) the possible filing of charges against four Facebook accounts that allegedly claimed the Supreme Court had blocked the arrest of Senator Ronald “Bato” Dela Rosa

key_actors.name: Office of the Solicitor General (OSG)
key_actors.stated_position: Urges the Supreme Court to deny petitions of Senator Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa, asserting that the law must prevail to ensure justice for the deceased.

key_actors.name: Sen. Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa
key_actors.stated_position: Filed petitions before the Supreme Court to block the implementation of the ICC arrest warrant against him; his lawyer states he is not a 'fugitive from justice' as the Supreme Court decision is provisional.

key_actors.name: Rodrigo Duterte
key_actors.stated_position: Subject of an ICC trial, whose lawyer requested a re-examination of his health and capacity to face trial, and whose appeal of confirmed charges was rejected by the ICC.

key_actors.name: Melvin Matibag
key_actors.stated_position: Stated that several more individuals may soon face ICC arrest warrants and insisted that the warrant against Sen. Bato dela Rosa cannot be served by a lawyer.

key_actors.name: Atty. Israelito Torreon
key_actors.role: Lawyer for Sen. Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa
key_actors.stated_position: Maintains that his client is not a 'fugitive from justice' because the Supreme Court decision is provisional and the warrant cannot yet be implemented.

key_actors.name: Alan Peter Cayetano
key_actors.stated_position: Called the war on drugs of former President Rodrigo Duterte's administration a 'pro-life campaign'.

key_actors.name: Baste Duterte
key_actors.stated_position: Made a 'bad joke' about Sen. Bato dela Rosa getting arrested by the ICC.

key_actors.name: Atty. Kristina Conti
key_actors.role: ICC Assistant to Counsel, legal counsel of EJK victims
key_actors.stated_position: Discussed the status conference at the ICC regarding former Pres. Duterte.

key_actors.name: Presidential Communications Office (PCO)
key_actors.stated_position: Endorsed to the DOJ the possible filing of charges against Facebook accounts for allegedly spreading misinformation regarding Senator Ronald “Bato” Dela Rosa's arrest.

factual_claims.claim: The Office of the Solicitor General (OSG) submitted its comment on two petitions filed by the camp of Sen. Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa before the Supreme Court.
The OSG urged the Supreme Court to deny petitions of Senator Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa.
The OSG asserts that the law must prevail to ensure that justice is achieved for the deceased.
The new lawyer of former President Rodrigo Duterte is requesting a re-examination of his health and capacity to face trial before setting a date for the start of the ICC trial.
ICC prosecution eyes 20-30 witnesses to testify orally in Duterte trial.
Over 13,000 evidence items are still pending review in the Duterte trial.
The International Criminal Court rejected former President Rodrigo Duterte’s request to appeal the confirmation of charges against him.
The Philippine National Police's tracker teams are now ready to pursue former agency chief Senator Ronald dela Rosa.
NBI Director Melvin Matibag says they received information indicating that several more individuals may soon face arrest warrants from the ICC.
NBI Director Melvin Matibag insisted that the warrant of arrest against Sen. Bato dela Rosa cannot be served by a lawyer.
Atty. Israelito Torreon explained that the Supreme Court decision regarding Sen. dela Rosa's petitions is merely provisional.
Senate President Alan Peter Cayetano called the war on drugs of former President Rodrigo Duterte's administration a 'pro-life campaign'.
Davao City Mayor Baste Duterte made a 'bad joke' about Sen. Bato dela Rosa getting arrested by the ICC.
The National Union of Journalists of the Philippines (NUJP) is calling for justice for the slain radio broadcaster Nestor Micator.
The Partido Lakas ng Masa called for justice for the victims of the war on drugs and the arrest of Sen. Bato dela Rosa.

The Presidential Communications Office (PCO) endorsed to the Department of Justice (DOJ) the possible filing of charges against four Facebook accounts that allegedly claimed the Supreme Court had blocked the arrest of Senator Ronald “Bato” Dela Rosa.

disputed_claims.claim: Sen. Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa's actions placed him within the definition of a fugitive, and he should be hunted down and arrested.
disputed_claims.competing_accounts: Atty. Israelito Torreon, lawyer for Sen. dela Rosa, states it is wrong to call his client a 'fugitive from justice' because the Supreme Court decision is merely provisional and there is no ruling that the warrant can already be implemented.

disputed_claims.claim: The warrant of arrest from the ICC against Sen. Bato dela Rosa could be served by a lawyer.
disputed_claims.competing_accounts: NBI Director Melvin Matibag insisted that the warrant of arrest against Sen. Bato dela Rosa cannot be served by a lawyer.

disputed_claims.claim: The Supreme Court had blocked the arrest of Senator Ronald 'Bato' Dela Rosa.
disputed_claims.competing_accounts: The Presidential Communications Office (PCO) is pursuing charges against Facebook accounts for allegedly spreading this claim as misinformation.

competing_frames.proponents: Office of the Solicitor General (OSG), ICC prosecution, Atty. Kristina Conti, Partido Lakas ng Masa, Veteran lawyer Lorna Kapunan, those calling for arrest
competing_frames.summary: This frame emphasizes the need for legal accountability for alleged crimes, particularly extrajudicial killings, and seeks justice for victims through international legal mechanisms like the ICC.

competing_frames.frame: Defense of Senator Dela Rosa / Questioning ICC Enforcement
competing_frames.proponents: Sen. Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa's camp, Atty. Israelito Torreon
competing_frames.summary: This frame challenges the legality or immediate implementation of the ICC arrest warrant, arguing against the 'fugitive' label and questioning the basis for immediate arrest.

competing_frames.proponents: Senate President Alan Peter Cayetano, Davao City Mayor Baste Duterte
competing_frames.summary: This frame re-characterizes the 'war on drugs' as a 'pro-life campaign' and uses political commentary to downplay or joke about the legal consequences faced by those involved.
This frame highlights efforts to manipulate public narratives around human rights abuses and accountability, including government actions against alleged misinformation regarding the ICC arrest.
